Back to Tools

Loan Closure Document Checklist

Find out exactly which documents, certificates, and updates you must collect from banks when closing a loan in India.

Critical Closure Warning

Simply paying your final EMI does not legally close a loan. Banks must update registries, release liens, and report a "Closed" status to credit bureaus. Forgetting to collect these documents can lead to property title issues, vehicle transfer rejections, or credit score drops years later.

Home / Mortgage Loan Progress0% Complete

No Objection Certificate (NOC) / No Dues Certificate (NDC)

Critical
Why It's Needed

Official statement from the bank confirming you have fully repaid the loan and no outstanding balance remains.

How To Obtain / Verify

Automatically dispatched by the bank within 15-21 working days of final closure. Follow up if delayed.

Return of Original Document Deeds (Registry/Sale Deeds)

Critical
Why It's Needed

The bank holds your original property sale deeds, conveyance deeds, builder agreements, and registry documents as security.

How To Obtain / Verify

You must visit the bank branch in person. Verify every single page of the original documents returned to ensure nothing is lost or damaged.

Removal of Mortgage Lien / CERSAI registry update

High
Why It's Needed

When you mortgage a property, banks register the charge on CERSAI (Central Registry). This charge must be updated to 'Satisfied'.

How To Obtain / Verify

Confirm with the bank that they have filed the 'Satisfaction of Charge' form with CERSAI. You can verify it online on the CERSAI portal.

CIBIL Report Status Update to 'Closed'

High
Why It's Needed

If the bank doesn't update the bureau, your credit report will still show an active loan, lowering your score or causing debt-to-income ratio issues.

How To Obtain / Verify

Check your CIBIL/Experian report after 30-45 days. If it's not marked as 'Closed' (make sure it doesn't say 'Settled' or 'Written Off' which harms score), raise a dispute.

Retrieval or Destruction of Post-Dated Cheques (PDCs)

Standard
Why It's Needed

Prevents misuse of physical cheques or auto-debits that you submitted when initiating the loan.

How To Obtain / Verify

Request the bank to return any unused post-dated cheques, or get a written declaration that they have been safely destroyed. Cancel any ECS auto-debit mandates.

Detailed Statement of Account (Final Ledger)

Standard
Why It's Needed

Serves as permanent physical proof of every single installment, interest rate change, and prepayment you paid.

How To Obtain / Verify

Download the complete statement from the net banking portal before closing the account, or request a stamped copy at the branch.
